meta.title: What's happening
meta.note: Curated cross-domain event log — the developments moving Mexico, each rewritten in plain English, dated, and linked to a first-party or record source. Auto-generated from the news wire on every refresh; raw or non-English items are quarantined, never published.

events.title: Audio leaks deepen feud between Baja California governor Marina del Pilar Ávila and former governor Jaime Bonilla
events.why: Ávila says the recordings, tied to a meeting with people she believed were US officials, were leaked by Bonilla, and the fallout threatens the ruling coalition's alliance with the PT in the state.

events.title: Federal government reports homicides in the State of Mexico fell 58% over 21 months
events.why: The figure, highlighted by the federal government, is one of the administration's central data points for arguing its security strategy is working in the country's most populous state.

events.title: USTR delegation to visit Mexico as US pushes to cut trade deficit
events.why: Jamieson Greer says the US wants to shrink its trade deficit with Mexico as USTR sends technical negotiators ahead of the USMCA review, signaling Washington will use the review to press for structural changes in trade flows.

events.title: Trump seeks to restrict Mexican exports in USMCA review, USTR reveals
events.why: The disclosure suggests the review process could result in new limits on what Mexico can sell into the US market, a shift from the deal's original tariff-free framework and a direct threat to export-dependent industries.

events.title: Rocha Moya case and organized crime complicate USMCA outlook
events.why: The decision to hold annual reviews instead of extending the deal for 16 years is read as political pressure from the Trump administration tied to corruption and cartel cases implicating Mexican officials.

events.title: Auto industry says sectoral tariffs block rules-of-origin talks with US
events.why: The head of Mexico's auto industry association says automakers paying a 19% tariff to Washington cannot negotiate rules of origin while sectoral tariffs remain in place, tying the USMCA review directly to the sector's cost structure.

events.title: US flags drop in American parts content in Mexican-made cars
events.why: A decline in US-sourced parts in vehicles assembled in Mexico raises concern in Washington and could become a bargaining point in the USMCA rules-of-origin review, affecting compliance costs for automakers.

events.title: New witness in fuel-theft probe says smuggling network sought help from Ojeda Durán
events.why: A witness in the huachicol fiscal investigation alleges the smuggling ring sought assistance involving a contraband ship from Ojeda Durán, the former navy secretary under López Obrador, deepening a case that has already implicated senior officials.

events.title: Sheinbaum sends bill to Congress to standardize feminicidio law nationwide
events.why: The proposal would unify how femicide is defined and prosecuted across Mexico's states, addressing inconsistencies that have long let cases fall through gaps between state and federal jurisdiction.

events.title: DEA hardens rhetoric on Mexico, Sheinbaum pushes back
events.why: Sheinbaum calls the DEA director's comments on Mexico's security situation unfortunate, an exchange that reflects rising friction between US anti-narcotics officials and the Mexican government over cartel enforcement.

events.title: Small gas station owners to meet SAT, Sener and Pemex over diesel price cap
events.why: Independent gas station operators want an explanation of how authorities set the 27-peso ceiling on diesel prices, a policy that affects their margins and could signal broader tension over fuel price controls.

events.title: Intentional homicides fall 58% in Edomex over 21 months, federal government says
events.why: The federal government highlights the drop in homicides in the State of Mexico as evidence its security strategy is working, though the figure comes from official sources touting their own results.

events.title: Sheinbaum backs Panama Canal neutrality, deepens ties with Mulino
events.why: Mexico's public support for the canal's neutrality protocol comes as Sheinbaum meets Panama's president to expand cooperation, a diplomatic move relevant amid US pressure over the canal's control.

events.title: US says Brazil tariff concessions favor India and Mexico in Section 301 probe
events.why: The finding ties Mexico into a US trade investigation originally focused on Brazil, suggesting Washington sees Mexico as a comparative beneficiary in ongoing tariff disputes that could affect future trade actions.

events.title: Mexico asks US to investigate migrant deaths in ICE custody
events.why: The Mexican government is formally requesting a US probe into the deaths of migrants held by ICE, adding a diplomatic dimension to a rising number of custody deaths reported this year.

events.title: Sheinbaum and Panama's Mulino agree to strengthen strategic partnership
events.why: The two presidents met to relaunch bilateral relations and Mexico voiced support for the neutrality of the Panama Canal, a diplomatic gesture amid US pressure over control of the waterway.

events.title: Mexican stock exchange expects fewer debt issuances after Pemex-driven surge
events.why: Long-term debt issuance grew more than 80% in the first half of the year, a jump analysts call unusual and tied largely to Pemex's return to bond markets, with volumes expected to normalize.

events.title: USTR pushes for tariffs and quotas in USMCA review to cut trade deficit with Mexico
events.why: Washington's push to add tariffs and import quotas to the USMCA renegotiation signals a harder line on Mexico's trade surplus, raising the stakes for the 2026 review process.

events.title: Auto sector shift hits Grupo México's rail business
events.why: Grupo México Transportes will lose about 1.5 million dollars a year in revenue after the Tacoma pickup's production changes, showing how shifting auto supply chains ripple into rail freight.

events.title: Private consumption in Mexico heads toward 15th month of expansion
events.why: Household spending data point to stronger performance in the second quarter, a signal economists watch closely for reading the strength of domestic demand and growth.

events.title: Sheinbaum launches transport stipend for university students
events.why: The government will deposit transport funds for thousands of university students starting next school year, part of a wider push on social spending tied to education access.

events.title: Prosecutors and Harfuch reportedly had leads on pilot 'Jando' months before extraditing him to US
events.why: Mexican authorities appear to have held information on a suspect for six months before handing him to US custody, raising questions about coordination and timing in security cooperation with Washington.

events.title: Mexico backs neutrality of the Panama Canal
events.why: Sheinbaum's public support for the canal's neutral status comes amid US pressure on Panama over canal control, a diplomatic stance that touches regional trade routes.

events.title: USTR says trade talks with Mexico advance while Canada concessions stall
events.why: Trade representative Greer's comments suggest Mexico is making more progress than Canada in pre-USMCA-review talks, a sign of how the three-country renegotiation is diverging by partner.

events.title: Trump orders push for tariffs, quotas and stricter rules of origin in USMCA review
events.why: The order comes as the US posts a trade deficit with Mexico near $197 billion, and signals Washington will use the review to squeeze Mexican exports rather than simply renew the pact.

events.title: Mexico publishes decree allowing entry of US troops
events.why: The decree formalizes a security arrangement with Washington and comes amid heightened US pressure on Mexico over drug trafficking and cartel violence.

events.title: Sheinbaum rejects Trump's order to resume immigration detentions during transit operations
events.why: The rejection signals friction between the two governments over migration enforcement even as trade talks continue in parallel.

events.title: Sheinbaum says USMCA review enters key phase, seeks deal before September
events.why: Her comments set a rough timeline for resolving the review, a process now complicated by US demands for tariffs and quotas on Mexican exports.

events.title: Sheinbaum rejects DEA accusations, asks US to focus enforcement domestically
events.why: The exchange reflects rising tension between Mexico and US drug enforcement agencies even as the two governments negotiate trade and security matters.

events.title: Apollo targets $20 billion in private credit investment in Mexico
events.why: The target marks one of the largest private credit commitments to Mexico from a global asset manager, a sign of continued investor interest despite trade uncertainty.

events.title: Mexico studies $4 billion financing plan for renewable energy
events.why: The plan would channel financing toward renewables as the government looks to diversify the energy mix and attract private capital into the sector.

events.title: Sheinbaum reveals 20 billion peso hidden cost to curb gasoline price rise
events.why: The disclosed cost shows the fiscal burden of keeping gasoline prices from rising, a subsidy that affects both public finances and inflation.

events.title: Sheinbaum sends bill to eradicate femicide
events.why: The legislative initiative would tighten the legal framework around gender violence, a long-standing demand from women's rights groups in Mexico.

events.title: GAFI warns organized crime increasingly launders money through cryptocurrencies
events.why: The financial crime watchdog says gaps in crypto regulation are being exploited by criminal groups to move large sums, pointing to a regulatory gap Mexico and other countries need to close.

events.title: Nuevo León leads investment announcements with $5.5 billion
events.why: The figure underscores the state's continued position as a top destination for new investment amid Mexico's nearshoring push.

events.title: Switzerland pledges $1.24 billion investment pipeline in Mexico
events.why: The pipeline adds to the flow of foreign capital targeting Mexico, part of a broader trend of European and Latin American investors betting on the country despite trade tensions with the US.

events.title: Calderón compares Mexico to Venezuela, criticizes judicial reform
events.why: The former president's remarks at an international forum add to the political debate over the judicial overhaul pushed through under the current government.

events.title: Guanajuato Congress launches initiative affecting Mexican migrants in the US
events.why: The state-level measure adds a new angle to Mexico's domestic response to migration policy, at a moment of tension over US immigration enforcement.

events.title: US designates Juárez Cartel and Los Viagras as foreign terrorist organizations
events.why: The designation opens sanctions and expanded prosecution tools against the two groups, extending a campaign that already labeled several major Mexican cartels as terrorist organizations earlier this year.

events.title: Sheinbaum says USTR will open offices in Mexico for USMCA review talks
events.why: The move keeps part of the trade review process based in Mexico as both governments negotiate ahead of a deadline this year.

events.title: Mexico files 17 criminal complaints in the US over migrant deaths in ICE custody
events.why: The legal action raises diplomatic pressure on Washington after a series of deaths of Mexican nationals in immigration detention, adding tension to the relationship as trade talks continue.

events.title: US tightens visa rules for foreign students, researchers and journalists
events.why: The new limits cap most academic and press visas at four years, part of a broader tightening of entry rules that affects more than two million foreign students and professionals, including Mexicans.

events.title: Mexico to invest $7.9 billion in 2,313 km of rail network
events.why: The plan marks one of the government's largest infrastructure commitments of the year, aimed at expanding freight and passenger rail capacity.

events.title: Mexican fintechs seek banking licenses to cut costs and boost growth
events.why: Moving from fintech to bank charters would let firms lower funding costs and expand lending, a shift that could reshape competition in Mexico's banking sector.

events.title: Government reports 58% drop in intentional homicides in Edomex over 21 months
events.why: The federal government cites the decline as evidence its security strategy is working in Mexico's most populous state, a claim likely to feature in its broader record on crime.

events.title: US farming investor refiles ICSID claim against Mexico
events.why: The renewed arbitration claim revives a legal dispute over Mexican government actions affecting US agricultural investment, adding to a list of investor-state cases pending against Mexico.

events.title: Mexico and Panama sign agreement backing Canal neutrality and security cooperation
events.why: The agreement aligns Mexico with Panama's position on Canal neutrality amid US pressure over the waterway, and adds a security cooperation component between the two governments.

events.title: Sheinbaum rejects DEA claim of a lethal connection between her government and cartels
events.why: DEA director Terry Cole's accusation escalates friction with Washington over security cooperation. Sheinbaum calls the statement political and unsupported and asks the agency to focus on drug enforcement inside the United States instead.

events.title: Mexico asks US state attorneys general to investigate deaths of migrants in ICE custody
events.why: Mexico's government is pressing for criminal investigations after 14 Mexican immigrants died in ICE custody and three more in agency operations since Trump's second term began.

events.title: Mexican Congress approves solidarity resolution for families of 17 Mexicans who died in ICE operations
events.why: The Permanent Commission's vote formalizes congressional condemnation of US immigration enforcement as the death toll among Mexican nationals in ICE custody grows.

events.title: Congress approves electoral reform
events.why: The reform changes rules governing how Mexico's elections are run, a structural change to the political system that will shape future contests.

events.title: Sheinbaum sends General Law against Femicides to Congress
events.why: The bill would create a national framework to standardize how femicide is classified and prosecuted, addressing inconsistencies across states that have long frustrated victims' families.

events.title: Mexico shifts USMCA strategy and names new ambassador amid Trump pressure
events.why: The move, reported by Politico, signals Mexico City is recalibrating its approach to Washington ahead of the treaty's scheduled review as trade tensions persist.

events.title: Restaurant food inflation stays above 8% for 19 straight months
events.why: The persistent gap between dining-out costs and headline inflation adds pressure on households and complicates the read on how quickly overall price growth is cooling.

events.title: Afore retirement savings stagnate after worst June for returns since 2022
events.why: Weak investment returns on the pension funds that manage most Mexican workers' retirement savings mean account balances barely grew, a sign market conditions are feeding through to household savings.

events.title: Sheinbaum backs permanent neutrality treaty for the Panama Canal
events.why: The declaration, made during Panamanian President José Raúl Mulino's visit to Mexico City, aligns Mexico with Panama's position as the canal's status draws renewed attention from Washington.

events.title: Vehicle theft in Mexico fell 16% over the past year, insurers report
events.why: The insurance association AMIS credits better coordination between authorities and insurers, a data point the government can point to on public safety even as other crime metrics stay contested.

events.title: Mexico to file complaints over deaths connected to U.S. immigration enforcement
events.context: Mexico says it will seek criminal complaints over 17 deaths connected to ICE, 14 in custody and three during operations. It will also pursue civil action against private detention operators.
events.why: The sharpest escalation yet in the confrontation with Washington over the deportation crackdown — Mexico will pursue complaints against those responsible for 14 deaths in ICE custody and civil suits against private detention operators.

events.title: Annual inflation falls to 3.37% in June
events.context: The INPC fell 0.27% from May. Annual inflation was 3.37%, down from 4.32% a year earlier.
events.why: A third straight monthly decline, back inside Banxico's 3%±1% band. This is the print that underwrites the pause and keeps real rates high, supporting the peso.

events.title: May U.S. goods deficit with Mexico widens to US$21.1bn
events.context: The U.S. goods deficit with Mexico was US$21.13bn in May, up from US$15.35bn in April.
events.why: The monthly U.S. Census ledger shows the bilateral goods gap widening by US$5.78bn from April.
